title: Content collection schema
intent: Astro content collections must be defined for series, commentary, and architecture entries with a status-based editorial workflow (idea/draft/review/published). All content lives in src/content/ with status in frontmatter — production builds filter to published only, dev server shows everything. Schema must be Zod-validated and type-safe.
steps: Create src/content/ directory structure (series, commentary, architecture)
Define series collection schema: title, number, publishedDate, description, tags, status (idea/draft/review/published), companionArtifacts (optional)
Define commentary collection schema: title, publishedDate, tags, description, status, tier (signal/architecture/deep-dive)
Define architecture collection schema: title, status, concern, patternType, tags, relatedProjects, updated
Configure content collections in src/content.config.ts with Zod validation
Implement build-time filtering: only status=published in production, all statuses in dev
Add seed entries at various statuses to verify filtering works

acceptanceCriteria.text: Content collections are defined for series, commentary, and architecture with status field
acceptanceCriteria.verify: test -f src/content.config.ts && grep -q 'status' src/content.config.ts && grep -q 'series' src/content.config.ts && grep -q 'commentary' src/content.config.ts && grep -q 'architecture' src/content.config.ts

acceptanceCriteria.text: At least one seed entry exists per collection
acceptanceCriteria.verify: ls src/content/series/*.md src/content/series/*.mdx src/content/commentary/*.md src/content/commentary/*.mdx src/content/architecture/*.md src/content/architecture/*.mdx 2>/dev/null | wc -l | grep -qv '^0$'

groomingNotes: No blocking deps. Content directories (series, commentary, architecture) already exist in src/content/ — FEAT-001 only needs to create content.config.ts and the Zod schemas. Build-time filtering is the key behavior: production must exclude non-published entries. Seed content already exists in commentary/ and series/. Mark ready: all prerequisites are in place.

title: Base typography and prose styling
intent: Long-form prose must render with Literata serif at comfortable size (17px), line-height 1.75, max ~720px content width, and proper heading hierarchy using DM Serif Display. Code blocks use JetBrains Mono on elevated dark surfaces with amber syntax accents. This editorial typography is the key differentiator.
steps: Choose and load a reading serif font (Source Serif 4, Literata, or Newsreader) for body prose
Define prose styles: max-width ~720px, line-height 1.7–1.8, comfortable paragraph spacing
Style headings h1-h4 with DM Serif Display, clear size hierarchy
Style inline code with monospace on slightly elevated background
Style blockquotes, lists, and links with amber/gold accent for links
Style code blocks with dark elevated surface, proper padding, monospace font
Verify rendering with existing seed content markdown

acceptanceCriteria.text: Prose body text uses a reading serif font, not sans-serif

acceptanceCriteria: Prose content renders with max line width under 75ch
groomingNotes: Added verify commands for all ACs — prose width, heading font, and code block styles are all checkable via grep. These were previously unverifiable opinion statements.

title: Dark mode
intent: The site must render dark-first with very dark blue-black backgrounds (#0a0a0f–#111118 range), off-white text, and correct contrast ratios. Light mode is not planned. CookieConsent banner is currently styled for white background — it must be restyled for the dark surface. color-scheme: dark must be set on html so browser chrome (scrollbars, form controls) matches.
steps: Add color-scheme: dark to the html element in Base.astro
Restyle CookieConsent.astro for dark surface: dark bg, light text, amber accent buttons
Audit all components for light-mode assumptions (bg-white, text-gray-600, border-gray-200, hover:bg-gray-50) and fix
Verify contrast ratios meet WCAG AA for body text and headings on dark surfaces

acceptanceCriteria.text: Body renders with dark background and color-scheme: dark on html
No bg-white or text-gray-600 classes remain in built HTML

groomingNotes: Blocked by FEAT-019 (tokens). Body already has bg-[#0a0a0f] and text-gray-200 from FEAT-002. Remaining work: color-scheme: dark on html, restyle CookieConsent for dark surface (currently white bg, gray text, gray borders), audit all components for light-mode assumptions.

title: Navigation component
intent: Every page must have consistent site-wide navigation linking to all top-level sections, working on mobile and desktop with no JavaScript required for basic navigation.
steps: Create src/components/Nav.astro with links to all top-level routes
Include site title/logo linking to home
Add responsive mobile layout (CSS-only hamburger or always-visible compact nav)
Integrate Nav into Base.astro layout
Highlight current page in navigation

acceptanceCriteria.text: Navigation component renders on every page via Base layout

acceptanceCriteria: Navigation includes links to series, commentary, architecture, projects, and about
groomingNotes: Blocked by FEAT-019 (tokens) and FEAT-003 (dark mode) — nav must use the final color palette and dark surface. Replaced theater AC with behavioral checks on built HTML.

title: Design system tokens and color palette
intent: The design system must establish a dark-primary palette with very dark blue-black backgrounds (#0a0a0f to #111118 range), light text, and amber/warm gold (#d4a853 range) as the single surgical accent color. The palette signals craftsmanship and maturity — forged, not vibed. Replace the current primary orange (#b86b3a) with a refined amber/gold scale. Define surface hierarchy (background, card, elevated), text hierarchy (primary, secondary, muted), and accent usage rules in Tailwind v4 @theme tokens. Note: FEAT-002 already defined --font-prose, --font-mono, and --color-primary in @theme. This sprint extends those tokens with surface/text hierarchy and replaces the orange scale with amber/gold. Update .prose class colors to use the new tokens where hardcoded hex values exist.
steps: Define dark surface colors in @theme: background (#0a0a0f–#111118), card (slightly elevated), elevated (modals/popovers)
Define text color hierarchy: primary (off-white), secondary (muted light), muted (dim)
Replace primary orange scale with amber/gold accent scale centered on #d4a853
Define accent usage rules: links, CTAs, active states, code syntax highlights — sparingly
Define border/divider treatments for dark surfaces (subtle, low-contrast)
Document token decisions in CSS comments

acceptanceCriteria.text: Tailwind theme defines dark surface tokens, text hierarchy, and amber/gold accent scale

groomingNotes: Replaced theater AC (grep source CSS for variable names) with behavioral checks on built dist/ output. Tailwind v4 emits @theme tokens as CSS custom properties in the bundle — checking for the actual hex values proves the tokens were defined and compiled. Note: global.css currently has primary orange #b86b3a — this sprint replaces it with amber/gold #d4a853 per intent. The verify command will fail until the sprint implements the replacement, making it a real gate.

title: Responsive layout and container system
intent: The site must have a mobile-first responsive container system with ~720px max prose width for content pages and ~1280px max for index/grid pages. Generous whitespace. No horizontal scroll at any viewport. Content breathes on desktop, reflows cleanly on mobile.
steps: Define prose container: max-width ~720px, centered, responsive padding
Define wide container: max-width ~1280px for index pages and product grids
Set up responsive breakpoints for mobile (320px+), tablet (768px+), desktop (1024px+)
Apply containers in Base layout or as utility classes
Ensure generous whitespace between sections (consistent spacing scale)
acceptanceCriteria: No horizontal scrollbar at 320px viewport width
groomingNotes: Blocked by FEAT-019 (tokens must be settled before container system uses them). Note: .prose already has max-width: 720px from FEAT-002. This sprint adds the wide container (1280px) for index pages and ensures responsive padding/breakpoints.

title: Footer component
intent: Every page must have a consistent footer with copyright, navigation links, RSS feed link, and contact — completing the page frame alongside the nav header.
steps: Create src/components/Footer.astro
Include copyright with current year
Add links to key pages and RSS feed
Add contact email or link
Integrate Footer into Base.astro layout

acceptanceCriteria.text: Footer renders on every page via Base layout

groomingNotes: Replaced theater AC (grep for Footer import in Base.astro source) with behavioral check on built HTML. Astro static output inlines component HTML so grepping dist/index.html for the footer element is a reliable behavioral gate.
